artifact.content_preview:
From: noc-team@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Network Operations Centre — Monitoring Tooling Question
Date: March 7, 2026

Hi Alice,

During our review of the Site A migration runbook, our NOC team raised
a question that we wanted to flag to the project.

Currently, the NOC uses SolarWinds NPM for network monitoring across all
GC infrastructure. As part of the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment, we
will need to ensure that the new switches are properly onboarded into
the SolarW

extracted_tasks.text: Determine if the SolarWinds NPM upgrade should be included in NGIS Phase 3 or handled separately.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Should the NPM upgrade be included as part of the NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities? Or should it be handled separately?

extracted_tasks.text: Onboard new Catalyst 9500 switches into the SolarWinds platform with polling intervals and alert thresholds.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: we will need to ensure that the new switches are properly onboarded into the SolarWinds platform

extracted_tasks.text: Upgrade SolarWinds NPM module to version 2024.1 to support Catalyst 9500 platform.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: SolarWinds' support for the Catalyst 9500 platform requires upgrading the NPM module to version 2024.1

metadata.subject: Network Operations Centre — Monitoring Tooling Question

alerts.detected_ask: Should the SolarWinds NPM module upgrade to version 2024.1 (including configuration and database schema changes) be included as part of NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities?
alerts.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative'
alerts.suggested_action: The Project Manager should formally reject inclusion of the SolarWinds NPM upgrade within NGIS Phase 3 scope, citing the explicit out-of-scope exclusion. A formal scope clarification memo or decision record should be issued to the NOC team and Alice Tran confirming this boundary. The PM should simul

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.35,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "Should the SolarWinds NPM module upgrade to version 2024.1 (including configuration and database schema changes) be included as part of NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities?",
  "baseline_reference": "OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative'",
  "suggested_action": "The Project Manager should formally reject inclusion of the SolarWinds NPM upgrade within NGIS Phase 3 scope, citing the explicit out-of-scope exclusion. A formal scope clarification memo or decision record should be issued to the NOC team and Alice Tran confirming this boundary. The PM should simultaneously escalate to the Director General of IT Infrastructure and the NOC initiative owner to expedite the separate NPM upgrade initiative, given the identified monitoring gap risk during the post-migration window (Go-Live D-006: April 30, 2026; Post-Migration Validation D-007: May 31, 2026). A risk entry should be logged in the project risk register capturing the monitoring gap as an external dependency risk, with the NOC initiative owner assigned as the risk owner. If the separate initiative cannot be resourced in time, a formal Change Request with impact assessment (scope, budget, schedule) must be submitted for DG-IT approval before any NPM upgrade work is absorbed into this project.",
  "reasoning": "The scope baseline explicitly and unambiguously excludes 'network monitoring tool upgrades,' naming NOC monitoring platform upgrades as deferred to a separate initiative. The SolarWinds NPM upgrade to version 2024.1 — including configuration changes and database schema modifications — falls squarely within that exclusion. While the NOC team's concern is operationally legitimate (a monitoring gap post-migration is a real risk), the framing of the question as 'should this be included in Phase 3' constitutes a scope expansion request. The alignment score is low (0.35) rather than zero because the underlying trigger — onboarding new Catalyst 9500 switches into existing monitoring — is a natural consequence of in-scope switch deployment work, and the NOC team has correctly self-identified the potential conflict and deferred to project judgment rather than assuming inclusion. The basic device onboarding/polling configuration within the existing SolarWinds instance may be defensible as in-scope operational handoff; however, the NPM software upgrade itself is not. These two activities must be clearly separated in any response to the NOC team."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Should the SolarWinds NPM module upgrade to version 2024.1 (including configuration and database schema changes) be included as part of NGIS Phase 3 network migration activities?
parsed_json.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative'
parsed_json.suggested_action: The Project Manager should formally reject inclusion of the SolarWinds NPM upgrade within NGIS Phase 3 scope, citing the explicit out-of-scope exclusion. A formal scope clarification memo or decision record should be issued to the NOC team and Alice Tran confirming this boundary. The PM should simultaneously escalate to the Director General of IT Infrastructure and the NOC initiative owner to expedite the separate NPM upgrade initiative, given the identified monitoring gap risk during the post-migration window (Go-Live D-006: April 30, 2026; Post-Migration Validation D-007: May 31, 2026). A risk entry should be logged in the project risk register capturing the monitoring gap as an external dependency risk, with the NOC initiative owner assigned as the risk owner. If the separate initiative cannot be resourced in time, a formal Change Request with impact assessment (scope, budget, schedule) must be submitted for DG-IT approval before any NPM upgrade work is absorbed into this project.
parsed_json.reasoning: The scope baseline explicitly and unambiguously excludes 'network monitoring tool upgrades,' naming NOC monitoring platform upgrades as deferred to a separate initiative. The SolarWinds NPM upgrade to version 2024.1 — including configuration changes and database schema modifications — falls squarely within that exclusion. While the NOC team's concern is operationally legitimate (a monitoring gap post-migration is a real risk), the framing of the question as 'should this be included in Phase 3' constitutes a scope expansion request. The alignment score is low (0.35) rather than zero because the underlying trigger — onboarding new Catalyst 9500 switches into existing monitoring — is a natural consequence of in-scope switch deployment work, and the NOC team has correctly self-identified the potential conflict and deferred to project judgment rather than assuming inclusion. The basic device onboarding/polling configuration within the existing SolarWinds instance may be defensible as in-scope operational handoff; however, the NPM software upgrade itself is not. These two activities must be clearly separated in any response to the NOC team.
